purpose: Gene-specific rules for building a null-only feed from public APIs and curated Nulls Project records.

defaultFilter.strictNullOnly: Keep true no-function, whole-gene deletion, biallelic/hemizygous deficiency, and named no-function alleles. Keep severe low-function only when the gene record is explicitly deficiency-like and label it as review.

includeMechanisms: gene conversions or hybrids with no-function assignment
strong inhibitor phenoconversion as induced functional null
reviewMechanisms: decreased-function alleles such as CYP2D6*10 or CYP2D6*41
consumer SNP-only calls missing copy number
geneSpecificRules: Treat CYP2D6 as structural-variant-sensitive; do not trust SNP-only null calls.
Separate inherited no-function from medically induced functional null.
Keep poor-metabolizer labels only when they can be translated into no/minimal function or explicitly marked as phenotype buckets.
Track endogenous CNS routes separately from drug metabolism.
For null-only mode, include no-function alleles and poor-metabolizer genotypes.
Keep CYP2C19*17 only as an excluded directionality contrast.
Separate clopidogrel activation from other substrate exposure effects.

includeMechanisms: high-impact pLoF with DPD deficiency evidence
excludeMechanisms: fluoropyrimidine risk annotations without DPYD functional loss
geneSpecificRules: Null-only mode should include true no-function and biallelic deficiency.
Severe decreased-function alleles remain in the feed but must be labeled review rather than strict true null.
Do not reduce DPYD to a single variant panel.

includeMechanisms: TPMT no-function or nonfunctional star alleles
reviewMechanisms: enzyme activity affected by non-genetic context
geneSpecificRules: Separate TPMT deficiency from NUDT15 toxicity defense.
Keep enzyme activity phenotype when genotype is incomplete.
Label heterozygous decreased function separately from biallelic null-like deficiency.

includeMechanisms: NUDT15 loss-of-function or severe low-function alleles
geneSpecificRules: Do not frame NUDT15 as relevant only to one ancestry group.
Keep functional toxicity-defense mechanism visible even when the variant is not a classic truncating LoF.
Interpret alongside TPMT for thiopurine contexts.

reviewMechanisms: variant labels without quantitative enzyme activity
excludeMechanisms: blanket drug fear without oxidative-stress mechanism
geneSpecificRules: Prefer quantitative enzyme activity where available.
Represent X-linked mosaicism explicitly.
Separate drug, infection, chemical, and fava-bean exposure contexts.
Keep acquired low cholinesterase causes separate from inherited null/deficiency.
Label succinylcholine and mivacurium exposure contexts explicitly.
Prefer enzyme activity and dibucaine number context when available.
Default strict-null filters should not overcall common UGT1A1*28 as a true null.
Keep severe deficiency and low-function capacity reduction as separate layers.
Separate benign bilirubin biology from irinotecan/SN-38 toxicity context.

includeMechanisms: human LoF associated with lower LDL-C
reviewMechanisms: population association without directionally proven LoF
geneSpecificRules: Preserve directionality: LoF protective, gain of function harmful.
Do not turn population-level protection into personal medical advice.
Use gnomAD pLoF and Open Targets-style evidence for expansion.

reviewMechanisms: low lipid phenotype without ANGPTL3 mechanism
excludeMechanisms: association-only lipid signal without LoF direction
geneSpecificRules: Separate biallelic familial combined hypolipidemia from heterozygous protective lipid effects.
Use LoF directionality as the core filter.
Do not collapse low lipid phenotype into a monogenic diagnosis.
